title: Feel the Sensation
html:
What we live for, no matter what higher-order purpose we think of, is all about seeking and experiencing the sensation. Strip away the stories we tell about meaning and ambition, and underneath them is something simpler: we are chasing a feeling, and the purposes are mostly the routes we take to get there.

The emotional sensations, the core “good” feelings that you (up to a certain degree) can deliberately choose to pursue, are the magnetic force of My Compass. Naming them matters, because once you know which feelings actually pull you, you can orient by them instead of drifting. For me, those are:

  • The fulfillment of the “this is it” moment. When I enjoy life as it is. When the present is all I have, and it is enough — no part of me reaching elsewhere.

  • The feeling of moving forward, knowing that the mission is far away, even when the direction, or what’s at the end, is unknown; but I know I’m moving forward (usually more than I feel). The certainty is not about the destination — it is about the motion itself.

  • Serenity — trust that everything is fine, I’m doing fine. A big part of this comes from Life Running Digital Engine, which quietly handles the things that would otherwise nag at me.

    • You can feel the imbalance happening. By instinct, you feel the urge to restore the balance; you like things balanced.
    • You feel the debts accumulated, and you like releasing them.
    • Where there is expectation, there is debt. Every open expectation, your own or someone else’s, sits on the books until it is settled.

(A way of sensing I share with a friend's notes.)

  • Sense

backlinks.title: The heuristics for what to do now
